Encoder Click: Turning the encoder may generate a short tone burst, or be silent.
Tone Pitch: The frequency of the side tone, in Hz. A series of tones between 233 and 932 Hz, corresponding to the musical notes of the F major scale from Bb3 to Bb5 (2 octaves).
Time-out: If the time specified passes without any display updates, the device will go into deep sleep mode. Restart by pressing the FN button.
Quick Start: Bypass the initial menu selection: at startup the device will immediately begin executing the mode that had been in effect before last shutdown.
Output Case: Changes the case of decoded characters on the display (and on serial output via USB, and on Bluetooth keyboard output) from lower case to UPPER CASE.
Headphone Output: (M32 Pocket only) Determines what happens when headphones or another device are connected. 'Phones' = headphones with speaker muted. 'line-out' = headphone output at full volume, speaker normal. 'l-o: Var. Vol.' = headphone output at set volume. 'l-o: Lsp Muted' = headphone output full volume, speaker muted. Caution: Never plug in headphones when using line-out options!
Theme: (Color screen devices only, e.g. M32Pocket) Set a color theme for the display.
BLT Kbd Output: Defines what is sent via Bluetooth keyboard. VBand allows use as a VBand dongle. Decoded sends decoded characters via Bluetooth. Generic Kbd sends Enter for \ (new message) and Backspace for 8 dits. The M32 appears as a US keyboard (QWERTY).
Serial Output: Controls what is sent to the serial port (USB). Distinction between keyed characters (iambic keyer), decoded characters (CW decoder or straight key), and generated characters (CW Generator, LoRa/WiFi receiver). 'All' sends everything. The M32 Serial Protocol also uses this port.
Defines which paddle side is for dits and which for dahs.
Reverses the polarity of an external paddle. Use this if your external paddle is wired the wrong way, so that dots and dashes of internal and external paddle are all on the same side.
Keyer Mode: Sets the keying mode: Iambic A, Iambic B, Ultimatic, Non-Squeeze, or Straight Key.
CurtisB DahT%: Timing in Curtis B mode for dahs (0–100%). Also influences behavior in Ultimatic mode. Recommended range: 35–55%.
CurtisB DitT%: Timing in Curtis B mode for dits (0–100%). Also influences behavior in Ultimatic mode. Recommended range: 55–95%.
AutoChar Spc: Minimum spacing between characters (Automatic Character Spacing). Off, or 2–4 dit lengths minimum.
Latency: How long after generating the current element (dot or dash) the paddles will be deaf. 0% = must release paddle while element is still on. Up to 87.5% = paddles react only after 7/8 of a dot length.
Koch Sequence: Determines the sequence of characters for the Koch method. M32 (native/JLMC), LCWO, CW Academy, LICW Carousel, or Custom Chars. ➜ See the Koch tab for lesson selection and character display.
LICW Carousel: Entry point into the LICW Carousel curriculum. Only relevant when Koch Sequence is set to LICW Carousel. Set this when you start a BC1 or BC2 course.
Interchar Spc: The time (in dit lengths) inserted between characters. Together with Interword Spc, this controls 'Farnsworth Speed'. Default: 3 dits. Range: 3–45.
InterWord Spc: The time (in dit lengths) inserted between words. Also affects the decoder's word boundary detection. Together with Interchar Spc, controls 'Wordsworth Speed'. Default: 7 dits. Range: 6–105.
Random Groups: For random character generation: which character subsets to include. Alpha, Numerals, Interpunctuation, Pro Signs, or combinations.
Length Rnd Gr: How many characters in each group of random characters. Traditionally 5, but smaller numbers may help for training. Can also be random within a range (e.g. 2 to 6).
Length Calls: Maximum length of generated call signs. Unlimited or max 3–6 characters.
Calls Region: Filter generated call signs by continent: All, Europe, N America, S America, Africa, Asia, or Oceania.
Call Prefixes: Filter out rare prefixes and only generate call signs with common prefixes.
Length Abbrev: Maximum length of randomly generated CW abbreviations and Q groups. Unlimited or max 2–6.
Length Words: Maximum length of randomly generated English words. Unlimited or max 2–6.
Max # of Words: After this number of words/groups, the M32 generates an AR (+) pro sign and pauses. Touch a paddle to continue. Ignored when Auto Stop is active. Unlimited or 5–250 in steps of 5.
Stop<Next>Rep: Stops generating after each word. Right paddle = next word, left paddle = repeat. Not compatible with 'Each Word 2x' — enabling one disables the other.
CW Gen Displ: How the CW Generator or Transceivers display generated/received content: Display off, Char by Char, or Word by Word.
Randomize File: If on, the file player skips a random number of words (0–255) after each word sent.
Each Word 2x: In CW Generator mode, each word is output twice for learning head copy. Three ON modes differ in inter-character spacing during repetition. Not compatible with Stop/Next/Rep.
Echo Prompt: How Echo Trainer prompts you: Sound only (best for head copy training), Display only (good for paddle input training), or Sound & Display.
Echo Repeats: How often a word is repeated after an error before generating a new one. 0 = always new, up to 6, or Forever.
Confrm. Tone: Whether an audible confirmation tone sounds in Echo Trainer mode. Visual OK/ERR indication remains regardless.
Tone Shift: Pitch shift for Echo Trainer response tone or transceiver transmit tone: no shift, up half tone, or down half tone.
Adaptv. Speed: If ON, speed increases by 1 WpM after correct Echo Trainer responses and decreases by 1 after mistakes.
Echo Speed Max: Maximum speed for keying input in Echo Trainer mode. If speed exceeds this (via encoder or adaptive speed), input speed stays at the maximum. No limit, or 5–50 wpm.
Key ext TX: When to key a connected external transmitter. Never, CW Keyer only (and Transceiver modes), Keyer & Generator, or Keyer & Gen & RX.
Generator Tx: Allows the CW Generator to transmit generated code via LoRa or WiFi. Useful for groups of learners. Caution: Always have an antenna connected when transmitting via LoRa!
Trx Channel: Virtual channel for LoRa or EspNow. Standard Ch or Secondary Ch. For EspNow this changes the WiFi channel (6 vs 1).
Bandwidth: Audio bandwidth of the CW decoder (Goertzel filter). Wide ≈ 600 Hz, Narrow ≈ 150 Hz. Center frequency ≈ 700 Hz.
Decoded on IO: Send decoded CW audio to the external audio I/O port (in transceiver and decoder modes). Ignored on M32Pocket.
